name: Beers vs. Blood Alcohol
description: Number of beers consumed and resulting blood alcohol content (BAC) for 16 students. Strong linear relationship. IMS Ch. 7, 24.
studyDescription: Sixteen student volunteers at Ohio State University each drank a randomly assigned number of 12-ounce beers, and their blood alcohol content (BAC) was measured 30 minutes later by a police officer. This was a randomized experiment with the number of beers as the treatment variable.

variableDescriptions.beers: Number of 12-ounce beers consumed by the student
variableDescriptions.bac: Blood alcohol content measured in grams per deciliter (g/dL), recorded 30 minutes after drinking

sourceDetail: OpenIntro IMS Ch. 7, 24, openintro::bac. Original data from a study conducted at Ohio State University.

context.parameter: slope of BAC on beers consumed

inferenceContexts.parameter: the population slope of the relationship between beers consumed and blood alcohol content
inferenceContexts.claim: there is a positive linear relationship between the number of beers consumed and blood alcohol content
